channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump says it was 'stupid' for Biden to let Ukraine use US weapons to strike deeper into Russia
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Monday suggested that he may reverse President Joe Biden's recent decision to allow Ukrainian forces to use American long-range weapons to strike deeper into Russian territory. Trump called the decision made by Biden last month “stupid.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: RFK Jr. meets with senators as questions swirl about Trump's pick to lead health agency
item.description: Associated Press (AP) — Robert F. Kennedy Jr. swept onto Capitol Hill late Monday, as the anti-vaccine health guru from the famous political family reintroduced himself to senators, this time as President-elect Donald Trump's pick to lead the nation's Health and Human Services Department.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Connolly bests Ocasio-Cortez in key vote to lead Democrats on Oversight panel
item.description: Rep. Gerry Connolly (D-Va.) on Monday claimed the first round of the two-step contest to lead Democrats on the House Oversight and Accountability Committee in the next Congress, besting Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DC councilman recommended for expulsion as he faces bribery charges
item.description: Washington, D.C. city councilman Trayon White could be removed from office after an ad hoc committee unanimously voted on Monday to recommend the council expel him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: March for Our Lives co-founder David Hogg launches DNC vice chair bid
item.description: Hogg, 24, cited Democrats’ losing ground with young voters in November as a motivating factor to run for the leadership position within the party.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Top Republican demands answers from Zuckerberg, accusing Meta of ‘shadow banning’ military content
item.description: Sen. Joni Ernst, R-Iowa, said in a letter to Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg that he needs to answer for purported "shadow banning" of military-related postings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he will consider pardon for NYC Mayor Eric Adams, says he was targeted for illegal migrant stance
item.description: President-elect Trump said he would consider issuing a pardon for New York City Mayor Eric Adams amid corruption charges alleging he took bribes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah chief says group lost critical arms supply route from Iran with Syrian ouster of Assad
item.description: Hezbollah lost its most important supply route from Iran through Syria with the fall of dictator Bashar al-Assad, the group’s chief admitted Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump allies push for more White House control over Congress' purse strings
item.description: House Republican Rep. Andrew Clyde, R-Ga., is leading the charge to give President-elect Donald Trump more say over how government funding is handled.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former FBI informant will plead guilty to lying about phony bribery scheme involving the Bidens
item.description: A former FBI informant is set to plead guilty to lying about a phony bribery scheme involving President Joe Biden and his son, Hunter, that became central to a Republican impeachment inquiry in Congress.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Journalists anticipate a renewed hostility toward their work under the incoming Trump administration
item.description: When Donald Trump takes office as president for a second time, many journalists anticipate a hostile climate toward them and their work.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden, Harris thank major Democratic donors and urge them to stay engaged after tough loss to Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden and Vice President Kamala Harris on Sunday thanked deep-pocketed Democratic donors who raised record sums in last month's election loss to President-elect Donald Trump and urged them not to lose hope and to remain politically engaged.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former longtime South Carolina congressman John Spratt dies at 82
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — John Spratt, a former longtime Democratic congressman from South Carolina who successfully pushed for a balanced budget deal in the 1990s but was unseated decades later when his district turned Republican, has died. He was 82.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More than 120 House Democrats send letter to Biden urging him to ratify Equal Rights Amendment
item.description: “We must continue our efforts to fully affirm and recognize the equality of rights for all people, regardless of sex, as part of our Constitution, a vital effort that has never been more urgent,” Congress members penned.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: 5 Senate Republicans who could face primaries in 2026
item.description: Republican senators are gearing up for the possibility of primary challenges ahead of the 2026 midterms as they seek to navigate the new political environment of a second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC Council speaker concerned about deportation plans
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- New York City Council Speaker Adrienne Adams has concerns about Mayor Eric Adams' goal to deport migrants who commit violent crimes in the city. Mayor Adams met with incoming border czar Tom Homan on Thursday to discuss President-elect Donald Trump’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: 5 things to know about Biden’s 1,500 clemencies, pardons
item.description: President Biden pardoned 39 people and commuted the sentences of nearly 1,500 others on Thursday, a sweeping move that comes after pressure from criminal justice advocates over the surprise pardon of his son Hunter Biden.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota cities take different approaches to recreational cannabis market
item.description: The Minnesota Legislature set most of the rules about cultivation and sale of recreational marijuana in spring 2023. However, the state left several key decisions within local control, including whether cities can allow more than a set minimum of shops.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Biden commutes the sentences for 1,500 people, the largest act of clemency in a day
item.description: The 1,500 people had been serving long prison sentences that would have been shorter under today's laws and practices. Three Minnesota women are among those to get their federal convictions pardoned.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith and Chandra Colvin, MPR News

item.title: Lombardo joins GOP governors 'fully committed' to Trump deportation threats
item.description: Gov. Joe Lombardo and 25 other Republican governors lined up in support of President-elect Donald Trump's immigration policies on Wednesday, calling for the deportation of "individuals who pose a threat.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kimberly Guilfoyle is the latest Trump family ally chosen for an ambassadorship
item.description: Kimberly Guilfoyle, who got engaged to Trump's son in 2020, is the latest appointee he's chosen from his family tree. There's a long and controversial history of presidents picking relatives for jobs.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Jacob Frey vetoes 2025 budget approved by Minneapolis City Council
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey announced Wednesday afternoon that he is vetoing the 2025 budget approved by the city council late Tuesday night. The budget had passed with enough votes to override a veto.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Florida state lawmaker files measure eliminating tuition aid for undocumented immigrants
item.description: Florida state Sen. Randy Fine (R) announced Monday he was introducing a bill to limit college tuition aid to undocumented immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Trump trolls Trudeau as 'governor' of Canada
item.description: President-elect Trump mocked Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, calling him the "governor" of the "Great State of Canada.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: As session nears, status of a Minnesota lawmaker facing criminal charges remains a flashpoint
item.description: Sen. Nicole Mitchell has upcoming court hearings in a burglary charge ahead of a trial that would start soon after 2025 Legislature gavels in. She’s resisting calls to step down, and a departure would leave the Senate knotted up.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News
